title: [InstCombine] Generalize `icmp (shl nuw C2, Y), C -> icmp Y, C3`

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on ult/ugt predicate transformations but does not explicitly mention ule predicate handling. The patch supports all unsigned predicates through the same transformation logic.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @icmp_ule_shl_nuw_c2(i32 %x) {
#0:
  %shl = shl nuw i32 8, %x
  %cmp = icmp ule i32 %shl, 32
  ret i1 %cmp
}
=>
define i1 @icmp_ule_shl_nuw_c2(i32 %x) {
#0:
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %x, 3
  ret i1 %cmp
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary examples all use i32 bit width. This test verifies the optimization works correctly with i64 types, which is not explicitly covered in the summary text.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @icmp_ult_shl_nuw_i64(i64 %x) {
#0:
  %shl = shl nuw i64 4, %x
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %shl, 16
  ret i1 %cmp
}
=>
define i1 @icmp_ult_shl_nuw_i64(i64 %x) {
#0:
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %x, 2
  ret i1 %cmp
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Edge case where C2 == C results in Div = 1 = 2^0. Summary discusses Div being power of two but doesn't explicitly cover the Log2(1) = 0 boundary case.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @icmp_ult_shl_nuw_c2_equals_c(i32 %x) {
#0:
  %shl = shl nuw i32 16, %x
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %shl, 16
  ret i1 %cmp
}
=>
define i1 @icmp_ult_shl_nuw_c2_equals_c(i32 %x) {
#0:
  ret i1 0
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors
